scenario: Ridge Cooperative issues 200 invoices per month through Camunda 8.8 Forms to its supplier network. Each invoice template loops over 15 records of line items and must show one currency symbol that lives on the surrounding object outside the items array, so the team needs the documented way to reach the symbol from inside the loop within 5 seconds per render.
question: How does a Feelers loop template reach data that lives outside the scope of the current iteration?

options.text: By writing an absolute path from the form schema root inside the loop body, which the runtime resolves against the original data object on every iteration
By using the parent accessor inside the loop body to reach data outside the scope of the individual items, such as a currency symbol on the surrounding object
By rebinding the surrounding data into a new local variable before opening the loop, and then referencing only that local variable from inside the loop body
By closing the loop, reading the outer value with a regular insert, and then opening a second loop that re-iterates the same array with the value passed in

a.text: Incorrect. Feelers does not expose an absolute path from the schema root inside a loop; the documented mechanism is the parent accessor.

b.text: Correct. Inside a loop, the parent accessor is used to reach data outside the scope of the individual items, for example parent.currency next to each item's price.

c.text: Incorrect. Feelers does not require pre-binding outer values; the parent accessor is the documented way to reach them directly from inside the loop.

d.text: Incorrect. Splitting the template into a pre-loop insert and a second loop is unnecessary; the parent accessor works from inside the original loop body.

explanation: Inside a Feelers loop, the parent accessor reaches data that lives outside the iteration scope, so a single template can mix per-item values with values from the surrounding object.
